description: Phase 5 (ADR 01066) browser session-reuse leakage gate for the Chromium family. Test A plants browser state (localStorage, sessionStorage, cookie) on an origin; test B runs on the REUSED, reset session (same context signature → drawn from the per-Appium-port reuse pool and reset via the CDP protocol) and asserts none of that state carried over. Because reuse is fail-closed, B falls back to a fresh session if the reset can't complete — in which case B is still clean and PASSes. So this spec never FAILs the gate spuriously; it only FAILs if a REUSED session leaks state (a broken reset). Chromium reuse needs a real GPU/display, so it runs only on headed Chrome (Windows/macOS); every other leg SKIPs. Concurrency note: with concurrentRunners>1 the two tests may land on different pool ports and not reuse — both are clean and PASS — so the leakage assertion has teeth only when B actually reuses A's session, which is the common single-runner / freed-port case on the headed legs.

tests.description: Plant localStorage, sessionStorage, and a cookie on the origin, then confirm they are set (so the assert test is meaningful).

steps.description: Plant state across the storage classes an origin persists.
Confirm the plant took effect within this session.

tests.description: On the reused+reset session, every planted state class must be gone.

steps.description: sessionStorage must be empty (fresh window + storage clear).
